channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alphabet Approves Performance-Based Compensation Package for CEO Sundar Pichai Worth Up to $692 Million Over Three Years
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, has approved a new three-year compensation package for CEO Sundar Pichai that could total up to $692 million, according to an SEC filing reported on March 7, 2026. T...

item.title: Heavy rains in Nairobi cause 23 deaths and disrupt transport and water supply
item.description: Nairobi is facing serious challenges after heavy overnight rains caused flash floods, killing at least 23 people. Rescue teams and emergency workers are still searching for missing persons and recover...

item.title: Translator J Devika Discusses Literary Translation, Politics, and Preserving Cultural Nuances
item.description: In an insightful conversation with Scroll, J Devika, an academic and feminist translator, shares her philosophy and approach to literary translation. Devika recently translated Manoj Kuroor’s novel 'T...

item.title: Nigerian Celebrities Wey Dey Gift Luxury Cars to Their Family Members Recently
item.description: For Naija entertainment world, e don dey common say celebrities dey show love to their family by splashing big money on luxury cars as gifts. This article talk about six recent cases wey celebrities p...

item.title: Investigation Reveals Firing by US Marine During Karachi Consulate Protest
item.description: The Joint Interrogation Team (JIT) investigating the March 1 violence outside the US consulate in Karachi revealed that a US marine fired in retaliation after protesters breached the consulate's secur...

item.title: Obama Delivers Eulogy at Rev. Jesse Jackson's Final Chicago Memorial
item.description: Former President Barack Obama delivered a moving eulogy at the final public tribute to civil rights icon Rev. Jesse Jackson held at a Chicago church on March 6, 2026. Jackson, who passed away at age 8...

item.title: Enugu State issues 90-day deadline for property owners to get building approvals
item.description: The Enugu State Government don give property owners wey never get building plan approvals 90 days to regularize their properties make e conform with the state planning regulations. According to public...

item.title: Trump threatens intensified strikes on Iran, claims apology from Tehran signals surrender
item.description: US President Donald Trump posted on Truth Social that Iran would be 'hit very hard' on Saturday, amid the ongoing conflict involving US and Israeli military actions against the country. He stated that...

item.title: Museveni takes over as EAC Chair during financial and security challenges
item.description: During the 25th Ordinary Summit of the East African Community (EAC) held in Arusha, Uganda’s President Yoweri Kaguta Museveni was officially elected as the new Chairperson of the EAC Summit of Heads o...

item.title: Toronto Police Warn Public About Phone Number Spoofing Scams
item.description: The Toronto Police Service (TPS) has issued a warning to the public about an ongoing scam in which fraudsters are impersonating police officers by using ‘spoofed’ phone numbers that appear to be legit...

item.title: Formerly Homeless Man Wins $1.4 Million Lottery Jackpot
item.description: A man from Colorado, known only as Johnathan G, who previously spent time in a homeless shelter, has won a $1.4 million prize in the state lottery after what he described as a tough day at work. Johna...

item.title: Philippines Implements Temporary Four-Day Workweek Amid Rising Oil Prices
item.description: The Philippines will adopt a temporary four-day workweek for all government offices starting Monday, as announced by President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., in response to rising fuel prices caused by the ong...

item.title: Pakistani Worker Dies in Dubai after Debris from Intercepted Missile Falls on Vehicle
item.description: A Pakistani driver tragically lost his life in Dubai's Al Barsha area on Saturday night when debris from an aerial interception struck his vehicle, according to the Dubai Media Office. This marks the ...

item.title: Increased Demand and Social Media Trends Driving Greek Yoghurt Shortages in Australia
item.description: Australians have recently been experiencing shortages of Greek yoghurt, particularly lighter and high-protein options. Major supermarkets like Woolworths, Coles, and Aldi report working closely with s...

item.title: Joint Security Operation Rescues 20 Kidnap Victims, Neutralises Suspects in Kaduna Forests
item.description: Personnel from the Anti-Kidnapping Unit of the FCT Police Command, working together with operatives from the Nigerian Army Guards Brigade, the Department of State Services (DSS), and local vigilantes,...

item.title: Government urged to implement long-term solutions to reduce flood damage
item.description: Heavy rains across Kenya have caused serious flooding, particularly in Nairobi, coastal regions, and western Kenya, leading to deaths, displacement, and property destruction. The floods have also disr...

item.title: Strengthening South Africa’s economy against global energy shocks
item.description: The recent escalation in the conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran is having immediate repercussions for South Africa, most notably in the energy sector. Brent crude has surged to $90 a...

item.title: US Court Convicts Pakistani in Iran-Linked Assassination Plot Against US Officials
item.description: A US federal court in Brooklyn has convicted Asif Raza Merchant, a Pakistani national, for his role in a plot allegedly linked to Iran to target former President Donald Trump and other US officials. T...

item.title: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie barred from Royal Ascot due to family controversy
item.description: Princesses Beatrice, 37, and Eugenie, 35, have reportedly been informed that they will not be allowed to attend Royal Ascot this summer due to their father, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's, and mother Sa...

item.title: Two Arrested After Suspicious Devices Thrown During Protests Outside NYC Mayor’s Residence
item.description: During protests outside Gracie Mansion, the official residence of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani, tensions between opposing groups escalated, resulting in the deployment of ignited suspicious devi...
